mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses predictive power during stable employment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and utilization, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases do not correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates reflect stronger aggregate economic activity, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved fundamentals.
failure_condition: GDPC1 increases fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.05 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5% forces central banks to maintain restrictive monetary policy, increasing real discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints, which compress corporate margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not predict negative 5-day SPX returns, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when inventory-to-sales ratios remain below historical averages.
